Hero.subtitle: Foundation Year · German Language · University Pathway
Hero.description: Study at our European campus, master German from A1 to C2, and earn direct admission to top German public universities — completely tuition-free.
Hero.trustLine: Official AP Tutor · Certified SAT Center · College Board Accredited · CEFR-Aligned German Academy

About.title: 20 Years of Excellence. Now in Europe.
About.description1: Founded in 2005, Target International School has grown from a single classroom in Tashkent into one of Central Asia's most respected and trusted educational institutions. Today, with 5 campuses, over 12,000 successful graduates, and alumni accepted into leading universities across the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, South Korea, and Europe — including TU Munich, Heidelberg, and Humboldt — we've built a reputation for academic excellence that delivers measurable results.
About.description2: Now, we're bringing that same proven methodology to Germany. Our new European campus offers students an unmatched opportunity: immerse yourself in one of the world's finest education systems, learn German from certified native-speaking instructors using CEFR-aligned curricula, prepare for the Studienkolleg (Feststellungsprüfung), and graduate directly into tuition-free German public universities. With full visa support, student housing assistance, and career counseling — your entire journey from Tashkent to Berlin is covered.
About.missionText: To bridge Central Asia and Europe through world-class education — empowering every student with German language proficiency, rigorous academic preparation, and a clear, structured pathway to tuition-free university education at Germany's top-ranked institutions. We believe every talented student deserves access to world-class education regardless of financial background.
About.visionText: To become the leading international educational gateway for students seeking affordable, globally recognized higher education in Germany — graduating the next generation of engineers, doctors, business leaders, and tech innovators who will shape the future of both Central Asia and Europe.
About.whyGermanySubtitle: Over 400,000 international students already chose Germany. Here's why you should too.
About.reason1Desc: Unlike the US ($50,000+/year) or UK (£15,000+/year), German public universities charge zero tuition fees for all students — including international students. You only pay a small semester contribution of approximately €150–€350, which includes a public transport pass for your entire city.
About.reason2Desc: Germany is home to 46 universities ranked in the global top 500, including TU Munich (#37 QS), LMU Munich (#54 QS), Heidelberg, RWTH Aachen, Charité Berlin, and Humboldt University. German degrees are recognized and respected by employers worldwide, from Silicon Valley to Singapore.
About.reason3Desc: After graduation, Germany grants an 18-month job-seeking visa to all international graduates — one of the most generous in Europe. Once employed, you can transition to an EU Blue Card, which offers a fast track to permanent residency within 21 months for high-demand professions like IT, engineering, and medicine.
About.reason4Desc: Germany is ranked among the top 20 safest countries globally. With over 400,000 international students from 190+ countries, you'll join a vibrant multicultural community. Located in the heart of Europe, your German student visa gives you visa-free access to all 27 Schengen countries — from Paris to Prague in hours.
About.reason5Desc: Germany is the world's 3rd largest exporter and home to industry giants like BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Siemens, SAP, Bosch, BASF, and Bayer. STEM graduates face an unemployment rate of under 2%, and IT professionals in Germany earn an average starting salary of €52,000/year. Germany actively recruits 400,000+ skilled workers annually.
About.reason6Desc: Average monthly living costs range from €700–€950 depending on the city — covering rent in a shared apartment or student dormitory (€300–€500), health insurance (€110), food (€200), and transport (included in your semester ticket). Many German cities also offer generous student discounts on museums, theaters, gyms, and cultural events.

Programs.title: Your Pathway, Your Choice
Programs.description: Whether you want to enter a German university through Studienkolleg, master the German language from scratch, prepare for international standardized tests, or build a career in business and technology — we have a structured, results-driven program designed specifically for your goals.
Programs.program1Desc: The Studienkolleg is a one-year university preparatory program officially recognized by all 400+ German public universities. Students complete intensive academic preparation in their chosen track, take the Feststellungsprüfung (FSP — university entrance exam), and earn direct admission to any German public university — including elite institutions like TU Munich, LMU, Heidelberg, and RWTH Aachen. Our students achieve a 95% FSP pass rate.
Programs.program1Feature2: Intensive German language training to B2/C1 level within 12 months
Programs.program1Feature4: Guaranteed university placement support with 40+ partner universities
Programs.program2Desc: Our German Language Academy offers intensive and super-intensive courses following the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR). Whether you're an absolute beginner or preparing for university-level fluency, reach your target level in as little as 6 months with our accelerated curriculum taught exclusively by certified native German instructors.
Programs.program2Feature1: All CEFR levels covered: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2
Programs.program2Feature2: TestDaF, DSH, telc, and Goethe-Zertifikat exam preparation included
Programs.program2Feature3: Small class sizes of maximum 15 students for individual attention
Programs.program2Feature4: Certified native German instructors with 5+ years teaching experience
Programs.program3Desc: As an officially certified College Board SAT Test Center and accredited AP Tutor, Target International has a proven track record of producing top standardized test scores. Our expert instructors use data-driven methods and real exam practice to ensure our students consistently outperform national averages.
Programs.program3Feature1: Official College Board SAT Test Center (Center Code: 694851)
Programs.program3Feature2: Accredited AP courses across 15+ subjects including Calculus, Physics, and CS
Programs.program3Feature3: IELTS preparation targeting Band 7.0+ with Cambridge-certified instructors
Programs.program3Feature4: 1-on-1 university application counseling, personal statement review & interview prep
Programs.program4Desc: A specialized program designed for students aiming to enter Germany's booming tech and business sectors. Study practical Business Administration, Finance, Data Science, and Software Engineering with project-based learning methodology, industry mentors, and real internship opportunities at German companies.
Programs.program4Feature1: Real-world project-based learning with German industry case studies
Programs.program4Feature2: Industry partnerships & guaranteed internship placements at German firms
Programs.program4Feature4: Modern tech lab with latest hardware, cloud computing resources & digital tools

German.title: Master German. Unlock Germany.
German.description: Our German Language Academy delivers structured, CEFR-aligned courses taught exclusively by certified native German speakers. Whether you're starting from zero or preparing for university admission exams like TestDaF or DSH — we'll take you from Hallo to university-ready fluency with a proven methodology and a 92% exam pass rate.
German.levelA1Desc: Learn basic greetings, introductions, numbers, and everyday phrases. Build your foundation in German grammar, pronunciation, and writing. By the end of A1, you can introduce yourself, ask simple questions, and handle basic daily interactions like shopping and ordering food.
German.levelA2Desc: Handle routine conversations about family, work, and daily life. Describe your surroundings, express needs, and navigate life in German-speaking countries with confidence. A2 covers past tense, modal verbs, and common sentence structures you'll use daily.
German.levelB1Desc: Express opinions, discuss current events, handle travel and workplace situations, and write structured texts on familiar topics. B1 is the gateway level required for most German residence permits, au pair visas, and many service-sector jobs in Germany.
German.levelB2Desc: Interact fluently with native speakers on complex topics. Understand academic lectures, write formal emails and reports, and participate in professional discussions. B2 is the minimum requirement for many German university programs and professional certifications.
German.levelC1Desc: Express ideas fluently, spontaneously, and precisely even on complex academic and professional topics. C1 is the standard requirement for most German university admissions and is equivalent to TestDaF 4 (TDN4) or DSH-2. At this level, you can write research papers and deliver presentations in German.
German.levelC2Desc: Achieve near-native fluency. Understand virtually everything heard or read, including idiomatic expressions and cultural nuances. Summarize and reconstruct complex arguments effortlessly. C2 qualifies you for academic teaching positions and professional careers conducted entirely in German.
German.intensiveDesc: 20 hours/week — 8 weeks per level. A balanced format combining thorough grammar instruction, conversation practice, and exam preparation. Fast-track your way to fluency.
German.superIntensiveDesc: 30 hours/week — 6 weeks per level. Our most popular and fastest format. Includes daily immersion exercises, one-on-one tutoring sessions, and mock exam practice. Ideal for ambitious students.
German.eveningDesc: 8 hours/week — 14 weeks per level. Designed for working professionals and part-time learners. Classes meet 3 evenings per week with flexible scheduling and homework support.
German.examPrepDesc: Specialized exam preparation courses designed to help you achieve the exact language certification required by your target German university. Our structured curriculum covers all four exam components — Reading, Listening, Writing, and Speaking — with weekly mock exams and personalized feedback. Our students consistently achieve a 92%+ first-attempt pass rate.

Opportunities.title: Where a German Degree Takes You
Opportunities.description: A degree from a German university doesn't just open doors — it removes walls. Germany's education system is globally ranked #3 for employability, and graduates enjoy some of the highest starting salaries and lowest unemployment rates in Europe.
Opportunities.uni1Desc: Germany's #1 university and a global powerhouse in engineering, natural sciences, computer science, and technology. QS World Ranking: #37. Home to 50,000+ students from 130+ countries.
Opportunities.uni2Desc: One of Europe's most prestigious research universities with 18 Nobel Prize laureates. QS World Ranking: #54. Excellence in law, medicine, humanities, and natural sciences. Founded 1472.
Opportunities.uni3Desc: Germany's oldest and one of the world's most distinguished universities (est. 1386). QS World Ranking: #65. World-renowned for medicine, molecular biology, physics, and philosophy.
Opportunities.uni4Desc: Home to 57 Nobel Prize winners including Albert Einstein and Max Planck. A beacon of academic excellence in the heart of Berlin. Pioneer of the modern research university model.
Opportunities.uni5Desc: Europe's leading technical university and a member of the IDEA League. Germany's top choice for mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, and technology research. 45,000+ students.
Opportunities.uni6Desc: A leading international research university and a member of Germany's Excellence Initiative. Renowned for humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and its world-class library system.
Opportunities.pathway1: 18-month post-study job-seeking visa — automatic for all graduates
Opportunities.pathway2: EU Blue Card eligibility for skilled professionals earning €45,300+/year
Opportunities.pathway3: Average graduate starting salary: €50,000–€65,000/year (STEM up to €75,000)
Opportunities.pathway4: Under 2% unemployment rate for STEM, IT, and engineering graduates
Opportunities.pathway5: Permanent residency eligibility after just 21–33 months of employment
Opportunities.pathway6: Direct access to 150+ Fortune 500 companies headquartered in Germany

Campus.title: From Tashkent to Berlin
Campus.description: 6 campuses. 2 countries. 1 mission — to give every ambitious student access to a world-class education and a direct pathway to Europe's finest universities.
Campus.germanyDesc: Our new European flagship campus — a purpose-built modern learning center in Germany, designed specifically for international students preparing for German university admission. Featuring state-of-the-art classrooms, a dedicated language laboratory, multimedia lecture halls, and collaborative student spaces in a central location with excellent public transport connections.
Campus.germanyFeature1: Smart classrooms with interactive digital boards
Campus.germanyFeature2: Dedicated German language laboratory with audio-visual resources
Campus.germanyFeature3: Student lounge, co-working spaces & quiet study rooms
Campus.germanyFeature4: Central urban location with S-Bahn & U-Bahn access
Campus.tashkentDesc: 5 established campuses across Tashkent, each specializing in different academic disciplines and serving over 2,400 students per year.
Campus.istirohatDesc: STEM Innovation Hub — Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics

Admissions.title: Your Journey Starts Here
Admissions.description: Applying to Target International is simple and straightforward. Our dedicated admissions team will guide you through every step — from your first application to your first day on campus in Germany. Most students receive a decision within 5 business days.
Admissions.step1Desc: Complete the online application form with your academic transcripts, a copy of your passport, and a brief personal statement outlining your educational goals and why you want to study in Germany.
Admissions.step2Desc: Our academic evaluation team reviews your application within 3–5 business days. Qualified candidates are invited for a 20-minute personal interview — available both online (Zoom) and in-person at our Tashkent or Berlin offices.
Admissions.step3Desc: Receive your official acceptance letter and enrollment confirmation. Our dedicated visa support team provides step-by-step guidance through the entire German student visa (§16b residence permit) application process, including blocked account setup (€11,904), health insurance enrollment, and embassy appointment preparation.
Admissions.step4Desc: Arrive at our campus, attend a comprehensive orientation week featuring city tours, cultural workshops, administrative setup (bank account, city registration, transport card), and meet your fellow students. Your new academic chapter in Germany begins.
Admissions.formDesc: Fill out the form below and our admissions team will contact you within 24 hours with next steps.
Admissions.success: Application submitted successfully! Our admissions team will contact you within 24 hours.

Footer.aboutText: Bridging Central Asia and Europe through world-class education since 2005. With 5 established campuses in Tashkent and our new flagship campus in Germany, Target International School provides a complete pathway — from German language mastery to tuition-free university admission.
Footer.rights: All rights reserved. Target International School.
